description: The whole of what the window may ask the client to do. This application is a governed window onto an instance. The window itself has no access to the machine: it cannot read a file, run a program, or open a picker of its own — this list grants it exactly one thing, listening for the events the client emits (socket frames, connection state, pairing loss, notification clicks), and everything else it does goes through the client's own handful of named commands. The client does work in folders on this machine, but the window cannot ask for it: that work arrives from the instance, on the socket, for a conversation the owner bound to a folder chosen through the system picker and agreed a level of trust for, and every path is checked to stay inside that folder. The picker plugin is reached only from the client's own command, never by the window; note there is deliberately no filesystem-plugin permission here, because the file work is done in the client's own code against paths it has resolved and checked, not handed to a plugin the window could reach. Adding to this list is a deliberate change and should be reviewed as one.
